name: farmOS Open-Source Farm Management API
what_it_does: Open-source farm record-keeping and management platform with JSON:API — enabling farmers, agricultural researchers, and extension services to self-host a farm data system for tracking crops, livestock, equipment, soil samples, field operations, and farm observations, with GIS/mapping integration, a standardized JSON:API for data exchange, and a growing ecosystem of modules for specialty agriculture.
use_cases: Self-hosting a farm data API via farmOS for on-farm data sovereignty and privacy
Recording crop and livestock logs via farmOS JSON:API for farm management automation
Integrating farm sensors via farmOS API for IoT data collection in precision agriculture
Building agri-data pipelines via farmOS for research and extension service data collection
Tracking field operations and soil tests via farmOS for compliance and record-keeping
not_for: Commercial farms needing SaaS support contracts (farmOS is self-hosted community project)
Large enterprise operations needing commercial support (consider AgriWebb or Granular)
Non-self-hosted deployment without technical resources (farmOS requires Drupal hosting)
best_when: A farmer, researcher, or agricultural cooperative needs open-source, privacy-preserving farm data management — where farmOS provides full data ownership, a standardized API for third-party integrations, and no vendor lock-in.
avoid_when: You need commercial SaaS support, don't have technical capacity to self-host Drupal, or need enterprise livestock traceability (use AgriWebb).

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials and authorization code flows. Bearer token authentication. Scoped access per resource type (farm_asset, log, quantity, term). farmOS v2 uses JSON:API (Drupal). Personal API keys via Simple OAuth module. No hosted API — self-hosted Drupal deployment required. Python farmOS.py client library available. JavaScript farmOS.js client available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source (GPL v2) — completely free self-hosted
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers; hosting costs only (Drupal hosting ~$10-50/month)
pricing.notes: GPL v2 license. Community project (farmOS Association formed 2019). Built on Drupal 9+. Created by Michael Stenta (mstenta). USDA NRCS funded development (conservation compliance use). Cornell University extension usage. Over 1,000 active farms self-hosting. Modules: farmOS Sensor (IoT), farmOS Map (GIS), farmOS Inventory, farmOS Livestock, farmOS Soil. JSON:API standard (v2) — interoperable with other JSON:API clients. OpenFarm data integration (crop data). OpenAgri standards alignment. Farm Hack community. GODAN (Global Open Data for Agriculture and Nutrition) member.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — no platform limits; server capacity dependent

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON:API (not custom REST) — follows JSON:API spec for filtering, sorting, pagination. OAuth2 with scopes. Self-hosted Drupal. Python and JS client libraries. No webhooks. User-controlled data residency. GPL v2 open source.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All resource reads fully idempotent (JSON:API GET). POST for log creation — check for duplicates. Asset reads idempotent by UUID. Full JSON:API spec compliance.
known_agent_gotchas: farmOS uses JSON:API spec — filtering syntax is ?filter[status]=active not ?status=active
Self-hosted Drupal required — no hosted API endpoint to call without your own instance
v1 (RESTful Web Services) vs v2 (JSON:API) are different APIs — ensure you're using v2
OAuth2 scopes per resource type — request all needed scopes at authorization time
UUIDs used for all resource IDs (not integer IDs) — Drupal JSON:API convention
No webhooks — poll API or use Drupal hooks for event-driven integrations

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on server config (user responsibility). OAuth2 with resource scopes. User-controlled data residency. GPL v2 open source — fully auditable Drupal codebase. Farm data (crops, livestock, locations) is commercially sensitive. Self-hosted means no third-party data sharing. GDPR compliance user-dependent on hosting setup.
